zh.lifeSummary: 當Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon於1160年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞阿斯圖迪洛時，他的父親Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano"35歲，母親Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla12歲。他和Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他於1203年在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞阿斯圖迪洛被列為governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…" 。他去世於1214年8月18日在摩洛哥享年54歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德華貝拿 德 度洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon于1160年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞阿斯圖迪洛时，他的父亲Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano"35岁，母亲Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla12岁。他和Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。他于1203年在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞阿斯圖迪洛被列为governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…" 。他去世于1214年8月18日在摩洛哥享年54岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德華貝拿 德 度洛。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Jorge, Miguel, Francisco, Mario, Pedro, Raul.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Ligia, Vasco, Wenceslao, Armanda, Fernandes.

Galician, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): topographic name from castro ‘castle, fortress’ (from Latin castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word. See also De Castro .

Italian and Jewish (from Italy): topographic name for someone who lived by a fortification, from castro ‘fortification’ (see 1 above) or a habitational name for someone from the town of Castro, the center of a Duchy in Lazio (destroyed after its occupation by Papal military forces in 1649). Compare Lo Castro .

variants.name: Alcazar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Francisco, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Julio, Rafael, Raul, Ruben, Alicia, Gustavo.

Spanish (Alcázar): habitational name from any of various places, for example in the provinces of Ciudad Real, Cuenca, and Granada, named with the word alcázar ‘citadel’ or ‘palace’ (from Arabic al ‘the’ + qaṣr ‘fortress’, a borrowing of Latin castrum; compare Castro ).

variants.name: De Castro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Armando, Mario, Alfredo, Jorge, Julio, Manuel, Ricardo, Rolando, Alvaro.
Galician and Portuguese: habitational name for someone from any of various places called with castro ‘castle, fortress’ (see Castro ), with the preposition de ‘of, from’.

variants.name: Castrillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jorge, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Guillermo, Ramon, Alberto, Alejandro, Ana, Benito.
Spanish: habitational name from any of various places, especially in León, named Castrillo, from castrillo ‘fort’, a diminutive of Castro .

variants.name: Castronovo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Santo, Cosimo, Giacomo, Graziella, Sal, Stefano.
Italian (southern): habitational name from any of various places called Castronovo, from castro ‘fortification’ (see Castro ) + novo ‘new’.

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Fernandez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Jorge, Miguel, Fernando, Mario, Jesus, Pedro, Francisco.  Portuguese Ligia, Joao, Armanda, Godofredo, Wenceslao.

Spanish (Fernández): patronymic from the personal name Fernando . The surname (and to a lesser extent the variant Hernandez ) has also been established in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some Jewish descendants retained the surname that their families had adopted as Roman Catholics.

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

nameMeanings.name: "el
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered short form of Terry or Terence , of recent origin. For the substitution of -l for -r, compare Hal .

nameMeanings.name: Castellano"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Miguel, Enrique, Armando, Carlos, Roberto, Guadalupe, Jesus, Luis, Manuel, Ruben.  Italian Carmine, Rocco, Angelo, Salvatore, Vito, Antonio, Carmela, Cosmo, Battista, Carlo, Dario, Enrico.
Spanish: habitational or ethnic name for someone from Castile.

Italian: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Castello, or a topographic name from castellano (from Latin castellanus), denoting someone who lived or worked in a castle. Compare Castello .

variants.name: Castellana
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Vincenzo, Ambrogio, Carlo, Carmelo, Caterina, Cesare, Giovanni.
Italian (southern, mainly Sicily): habitational name from a place called Castellana, notably the one in Sicily, or feminine form of   Castellano  .
Catalan: habitational name from any of the places called La Castellana, especially in Girona province, Catalonia (Spain).

variants.name: Castello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Adela, Arturo, Avelino, Dulce, Elena, Emilia, Enrique, Isadora.  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Vito, Aldo, Gaspare, Gaspere, Gino, Giuseppe, Lia, Luigi, Mino.

Italian: from castello ‘castle, fortified building’ (from Late Latin castellum), applied as a topographic name, a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word, or a metonymic occupational name for a servant who lived and worked in such a place. Compare Costello .

Catalan (Castelló): habitational name from any of the places called Castelló, as for example Castelló de la Plana, one of the main towns in Valencia province (Spain), or Castelló d'Empúries, a town in the Catalan district of L'Empordà.

variants.name: Castellan
meanings: Italian (northeastern): variant of   Castellano  .
Americanized form of Croatian Kaštelan or of its variant Kastelan: occupational name from kaštelan ‘castellan, fortress commander’, also ‘warden of a prison’. Compare Castelan 2.
French (southern): variant of Castella, a southern variant of Châtelain ‘lord (of the manor)’; see Chatelain . In part, it is probably of Italian origin (see Castellano , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Castellani
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Carlo, Guido, Annamarie, Antonio, Dante, Dino, Enio, Enrico, Franco, Gildo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Castellano  .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon was born in 1160, in Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spain, his father, Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano", was 35 and his mother, Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla, was 12. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo. In 1203, at the age of 43, his occupation is listed as governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…"  in Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spain. He died on 18 August 1214, in Morocco, at the age of 54, and was buried in Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon est né en 1160, à Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano", avait 35 ans et sa mère, Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla, avait 12 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo. En 1203, à l’âge de 43 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…"  à Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espagne. Il est décédé le 18 août 1214, à Maroc, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon 1160 in Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" 35 und seine Mutter Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla 12. Er hatte mit Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1203, als er 43 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…"  in Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spanien aufgeführt. Er starb am 18. August 1214 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Marokko und wurde in Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

it.Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Sp: Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spagna

it.Basílica de San Isidoro, León, Provincia: León, León, Castilla y León, Spagna
it.lifeSummary: Quando Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon è nato il nel 1160 nel Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spagna, il suo padre, Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano", aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla, aveva 12 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo. Nel 1203, all'età di 43 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…"  nel Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spagna. Morì il 18 agosto 1214, nel Marocco, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ja.Mosteiro de Valbuena, Valbuena de Duero,: スペインカスティラ イ レoンヴァラドリドヴァルブエナ ド ダエロ
ja.Of, , Gerona, Spain: スペインカタルニーアギロナ
ja.Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Sp: スペインカスティラ イ レoンプアレンシアアストゥデロ
ja.Monforte, Monforte del Cid, Alicante, Co: スペインコムニダド ベレンシアナアリカントモンフォート デル シドモンフォート

ja.Castilla y León, España: スペインカスティラ イ レoン
ja.lifeSummary: Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léonが1160年にスペインカスティラ イ レoンプアレンシアアストゥデロで生まれた時，父Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano"は35歳，母Estefanía Alfonso de Castillaは12歳であった。彼はJimena Gómez de Manzanedoとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。1203年，43歳の時，スペインカスティラ イ レoンプアレンシアアストゥデロでの職業はgovernor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…" と記録されている。彼は1214年8月18日にモロッコで54歳で死去し，スペインカスティラ イ レoンヴァラドリドヴァルブエナ ド ダエロにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon(이)가 1160년 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 파렌샤 애스투디로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano"(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla(은)는 12세였다. 그는 Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1203년, 43세의 나이로 그의 직업은 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 파렌샤 애스투디로에서 governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…" 이었다. 그는 1214년 8월 18일 모로코에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 발라도리드 발버나 데 드로에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon nasceu em 1160, em Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano", tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla, tinha 12 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo. Em 1203, com 43 anos, sua profissão aparece como governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…"  em Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espanha. Ele faleceu em 18 de agosto de 1214, em Marrocos, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon родился в 1160 г. в местности астудиллоу, пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания, его отцу по имени Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla, 12. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo. В 1203 году, в возрасте 43 лет,  он работал по профессии governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…"  в местности астудиллоу, пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания. Он умер 18 августа 1214 г. в местности Марокко в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности валбуена де дуиро, валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon nació en 1160, en Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano", tenía 35 años y su mamá, Estefanía Alfonso de Castilla, tenía 12 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo. En 1203, a la edad de 43 años, su ocupación registrada era governor in castrogeriz, palenzuela, dueñas and astudillo.  "pedro fernández de castro, hijo de fernando rodríguez de castro" donated "parte de balveis" to the order of calatrava by charter dated aug 1203[494].  "…pedro fernandi castellano regis maiordomo tenente extrematuram et transerram…"  en Astudillo, Palencia, Castilla y León, España. Murió el 18 de agosto de 1214, en Marruecos, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Valbuena de Duero, Valladolid, Castilla y León, España.
